title: Hot and Sour Soup

overview.description: A warming Chinese soup with chicken, mushrooms and tofu in a savoury broth balanced with vinegar, white pepper and chilli.

ingredients: 150g firm tofu, cut into thin strips
method: Bring the chicken stock to a gentle simmer.
Add the garlic, ginger and mushrooms.
Add the shredded chicken and tofu.
Stir in the soy sauce, black vinegar, sesame oil, chilli oil and white pepper.
Mix the cornflour with the cold water to make a smooth slurry.
Slowly stir the slurry into the soup until lightly thickened.
Reduce the soup to a gentle simmer.
Slowly pour in the beaten eggs while gently stirring to create fine ribbons.
Taste and adjust the balance of soy sauce, vinegar, chilli oil and white pepper.
Serve topped with sliced spring onions.

learning.did-you-know: The 'hot' in Hot and Sour Soup traditionally comes more from white pepper than from chillies.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to balance salty, sour, spicy and umami flavours while creating silky egg ribbons.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick, flavour-packed soup that uses homemade chicken stock and introduces classic Chinese flavour balancing with just a handful of pantry ingredients.
chapman-tips: White pepper provides most of the traditional heat, not chilli.
Taste before serving—the soup should be savoury, noticeably sour and gently spicy, with no single flavour dominating.
Chinese black vinegar gives the most authentic flavour, but rice vinegar makes a good substitute if that's what you have.
Add the eggs last, keeping the soup below boiling to create silky ribbons.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the soup base before adding the eggs. Stir fresh eggs through after reheating.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently and avoid boiling to keep the tofu intact and the egg tender.
